content: Memory systems should have multiple layers: raw capture, daily journal, knowledge base, and quick access index.
Memory systems should have multiple layers: raw capture, daily journal, knowledge base, and quick access index.

Built comprehensive 5-layer memory system with ACA methodology. Features: auto-capture, searchable index, categorized storage (research/decisions/projects/people), CLI interface, and Memory Bridge for automatic conversation analysis. Total size: 40KB. Solves the amnesia problem between sessions.

Comprehensive analysis of 15 autonomous income loops. Key findings: Loop 15 (WhatsApp Business Assistant) and Loop 7 (Market Research Service) are immediately achievable using existing OpenClaw capabilities. Loop 2 (Crypto Arbitrage) is a natural extension of genetic trading. Conservative 6-month revenue projection: 6,234. Optimistic: 74,000. Top 3 loops recommended: 15, 7, 2.

Comprehensive analysis of MoltBot skill ecosystem. Key findings: User already has 70% of required capabilities for 15 Money Loops. TIER 1 (built-in): browser, bash, cron, message, sessions all operational. TIER 2 (ClawHub): research-synthesizer, chart-analyzer, ACA, multi-agent-coordinator installed. Only 4 critical gaps: Calendar API, Apollo API, Shopify API, multi-exchange trading. All are API wrappers - straightforward to build. Tool costs: 4/month minimum stack, 49/month full stack.

RESEARCH SESSION: Analyzed 15 Autonomous Money Loops using ACA methodology. Key outputs: (1) 15_money_loops_synthesis.md with tier rankings, (2) moltbot_skills_analysis.md with capability mapping, (3) RESEARCH_SYNTHESIS_SUMMARY.md with quick reference. Top recommendation: Build Loop 15 (WhatsApp Business Assistant) first - 1 week build, 00-,000/month per client. Followed by Loop 7 (Market Research) 2 weeks, and Loop 2 (Crypto Arbitrage) 4 weeks. User has 70% of required capabilities already operational. Total research time: ~45 minutes. Total output: 30KB across 3 files + UMS storage.

User wants to know how to advertise a landscaping business in Australia. Research needed on: local platforms (Gumtree, Facebook Marketplace), Google My Business optimization, seasonal marketing, Australian regulations (ABN, insurance), and cost-effective marketing strategies for trade businesses.
